enrichment.short_description: Supermetrics is a marketing intelligence and data pipeline platform that automatically pulls data from more than 150 marketing, sales, and ad platforms into spreadsheets, BI tools, and data warehouses.
enrichment.seo_title: Supermetrics Review 2026: Features, Pricing and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Supermetrics review covering pricing, data connectors, BI integrations, and pros and cons for marketers who need automated reporting pipelines.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Supermetrics is a Finnish marketing intelligence and data pipeline company founded in 2013 by Mikael Thuneberg and headquartered in Helsinki, Finland. It automates the extraction of data from more than 150 advertising, marketing, sales, and analytics platforms (such as Google Ads, Facebook Ads, Google Analytics, and HubSpot) and delivers it into destinations like Google Sheets, Excel, Looker Studio, Power BI, Tableau, BigQuery, and Snowflake. The company serves roughly 200,000 organizations across 120 countries, ranging from solo marketers to large enterprises and agencies that need consolidated, automated reporting without manual exports. Supermetrics runs on a subscription model with four tiers: Starter (from 49 euros per month, one destination, three data sources, one user), Growth (from 199 euros per month), Pro (from 499 euros per month), and a custom-priced Enterprise plan with unlimited sources, data transformations, and a management API. Lower tiers include a bundle of AI credits and weekly, daily, or hourly refresh schedules depending on plan. The platform is bootstrapped-turned-VC-backed, having raised Series A funding in 2017 and Series B in 2020, and it acquired Dutch software company Relay42 to expand into a broader marketing intelligence platform. It is best suited for marketing agencies and in-house teams that rely on spreadsheet or BI-based reporting rather than custom data engineering.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Supermetrics
paragraphs: Supermetrics is a marketing intelligence and data pipeline platform that connects marketing, sales, and advertising platforms to the spreadsheets, dashboards, and data warehouses teams already use. It started in 2009 when founder Mikael Thuneberg wrote a script to pull Google Analytics data into Microsoft Excel, and the company was formally founded in Helsinki, Finland in 2013.

Today Supermetrics offers more than 150 pre-built connectors covering platforms like Google Ads, Meta Ads, LinkedIn Ads, TikTok Ads, Google Analytics, and HubSpot, and it moves that data into destinations such as Google Sheets, Excel, Looker Studio, Power BI, Tableau, BigQuery, Snowflake, and Amazon Redshift. Roughly 200,000 companies in around 120 countries use the product for automated marketing reporting.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: The core of Supermetrics is its connector library, which lets non-technical marketers pull raw campaign and analytics data on a schedule without writing SQL or building custom integrations. Data can refresh weekly, daily, or hourly depending on plan, and results land directly in spreadsheets, BI dashboards, or a data warehouse.

Beyond raw extraction, Supermetrics offers dashboard templates, a management API for governing multiple accounts, custom data import for blending offline or CRM data with platform data, and AI credits that power features like natural-language querying and automated insights across connected accounts.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Supermetrics is sold on four subscription tiers. Starter begins at 49 euros per month (39 euros per month billed annually) and includes one destination, three data sources, one user, and weekly refreshes. Growth starts at 199 euros per month with seven data sources, two users, and daily refreshes. Pro starts at 499 euros per month with ten data sources, three users, and hourly refreshes. Enterprise is custom-priced and adds unlimited sources and users, data transformations, full warehouse storage access, and support for multiple teams.

There is no permanently free tier, and pricing scales with the number of data sources, users, and refresh frequency selected, so agencies managing many client accounts typically land on the Growth or Pro tier rather than Starter.

features.title: 150-plus data connectors
features.description: Pre-built connectors for major ad platforms, analytics tools, CRMs, and social channels remove the need for custom API integrations.

features.title: Spreadsheet integrations
features.description: Native add-ons for Google Sheets and Excel let marketers pull live campaign data straight into familiar reporting templates.

features.title: BI tool destinations
features.description: Direct integrations with Looker Studio, Power BI, and Tableau support building automated executive dashboards.

features.title: Data warehouse loading
features.description: Data can be piped into BigQuery, Snowflake, and Amazon Redshift for teams that centralize marketing data alongside other business data.

features.title: Scheduled refreshes
features.description: Refresh cadence ranges from weekly on the Starter plan to hourly on Pro, keeping reports current without manual pulls.

features.title: Custom data import
features.description: Offline, CRM, or spreadsheet-based data can be blended with platform data for a fuller view of marketing performance.

features.title: Management API
features.description: Enterprise customers can programmatically manage connections and configurations across many client or brand accounts.

features.title: AI credits
features.description: Bundled AI credits power natural-language data queries and automated reporting assistance within the platform.

pros: Very broad library of ready-made connectors covering most major marketing and ad platforms
Lets non-technical marketers build automated reports without SQL or custom code
Works inside tools teams already use, including Google Sheets, Excel, and popular BI platforms
Scales from a single small-business user up to enterprise data warehouse pipelines
Established vendor with roughly 200,000 customers and a long operating history since 2013
cons: As of 2026 Supermetrics only offers annual billing on some plans, reducing short-term flexibility
Costs rise quickly as more data sources, users, or destinations are added
No permanently free plan for ongoing use, only trial access
Complex multi-source setups may still need someone with data modeling experience
Some advanced features and higher refresh frequencies are gated behind the pricier Pro and Enterprise tiers

faqs.question: What is Supermetrics used for
faqs.answer: Supermetrics is used to automatically collect marketing, advertising, and sales data from many platforms and deliver it into spreadsheets, BI dashboards, or data warehouses for reporting and analysis.

faqs.question: How much does Supermetrics cost
faqs.answer: Plans start at 49 euros per month for the Starter tier, with Growth at 199 euros per month and Pro at 499 euros per month; Enterprise pricing is custom.

faqs.question: Does Supermetrics offer a free trial
faqs.answer: Supermetrics has offered free trials on its paid plans in the past; current trial availability should be confirmed on the official pricing page since terms change over time.

faqs.question: What data sources does Supermetrics support
faqs.answer: Supermetrics supports more than 150 data sources including Google Ads, Meta Ads, LinkedIn Ads, TikTok Ads, Google Analytics, and HubSpot, among many others.

faqs.question: Can Supermetrics send data to Google Sheets
faqs.answer: Yes, Supermetrics offers native add-ons for Google Sheets and Excel that pull live data directly into spreadsheet reports.

faqs.question: Is Supermetrics suitable for data warehouses
faqs.answer: Yes, higher-tier plans support loading data into warehouses such as BigQuery, Snowflake, and Amazon Redshift.

faqs.question: Who founded Supermetrics
faqs.answer: Supermetrics was founded by Mikael Thuneberg, who formally established the company in Helsinki, Finland in 2013 after building early scripts connecting Google Analytics to Excel.

faqs.question: Is Supermetrics good for marketing agencies
faqs.answer: Yes, agencies commonly use Supermetrics to consolidate reporting across multiple client accounts, though larger agencies typically need the Growth or Pro tier to cover enough data sources and users.

use_cases.title: Agency client reporting
use_cases.description: Marketing agencies use Supermetrics to pull performance data from every client ad account into standardized dashboards or spreadsheets on an automated schedule.

use_cases.title: In-house marketing dashboards
use_cases.description: In-house marketing teams connect their ad and analytics accounts to build recurring executive dashboards without manual data pulls.

use_cases.title: Marketing data warehousing
use_cases.description: Data and analytics teams use Supermetrics to load raw marketing data into a central warehouse where it can be joined with sales and product data for deeper analysis.

features: All core and data warehouse destinations

enrichment.short_description: Apache Superset is a free, open-source data exploration and visualization platform for building dashboards and charts on top of virtually any SQL-speaking database.
enrichment.seo_title: Apache Superset: Free Open-Source BI and Dashboard Tool
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Apache Superset overview covering features, self-hosting, pricing, and how this open-source BI platform compares to managed alternatives like Preset.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Apache Superset is a free, open-source data exploration and visualization platform originally created by Maxime Beauchemin at Airbnb during a hackathon in 2015, when it was named Caravel. It was open sourced shortly after, entered the Apache Software Foundation Incubator in 2017, and graduated to a top-level Apache project in 2021. Superset is licensed under Apache License 2.0 and is built with Python and TypeScript. It lets users build charts and dashboards on top of SQL-speaking databases and data warehouses at scale, with support for enterprise authentication methods like OAuth, OpenID, and LDAP, a lightweight semantic layer, and a large library of visualization types including integration with Apache ECharts. Because it is a community-governed open-source project rather than a company, there is no vendor subscription fee to self-host Superset; organizations run it on their own infrastructure for free and only incur their own hosting costs. Preset, a commercial company founded by Beauchemin and backed by Andreessen Horowitz, Fathom Capital, and Redpoint Ventures, offers a managed cloud version of Superset for teams that prefer not to operate it themselves. Superset is best suited for data and engineering teams that want a self-hosted, SQL-native BI tool without per-seat licensing costs.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Superset
paragraphs: Apache Superset is an open-source data exploration and visualization platform capable of handling data at large scale. It began as a three-day hackathon project called Caravel built by Maxime Beauchemin at Airbnb in 2015, was open sourced soon after, and entered the Apache Software Foundation Incubator in 2017 before becoming a fully independent top-level Apache project in 2021.

Unlike closed-source BI tools sold as a subscription, Superset is free to download, self-host, and modify under the Apache License 2.0. It is written primarily in Python and TypeScript and connects to virtually any SQL-speaking database or data warehouse, making it popular with engineering-led data teams.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Superset supports building interactive dashboards and charts using a no-code chart builder or a SQL Lab query editor for more advanced analysis. It ships with dozens of visualization types and integrates with Apache ECharts, plus a plugin system for adding custom visualizations.

It includes a lightweight semantic layer for defining metrics and dimensions once and reusing them across charts, and it supports enterprise-grade authentication through OAuth, OpenID Connect, and LDAP, along with row-level security for controlling what data different users can see.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Superset itself is entirely free and open source; there is no license fee for self-hosting it on your own servers or cloud infrastructure, only the underlying compute and storage costs. Organizations that want a managed, hosted version without operating the infrastructure themselves can instead use Preset, a commercial SaaS built on Superset by its original creator, which is priced separately from the open-source project.

Because pricing for Preset's managed offering is set by a separate company and changes over time, teams evaluating a hosted option should check Preset's own pricing page rather than assuming it matches any figures published for the open-source project.

features.title: No-code chart builder
features.description: Users can create dozens of chart types through a visual interface without writing code.

features.title: SQL Lab
features.description: A built-in SQL editor lets analysts write and run queries directly against connected databases for ad hoc exploration.

features.title: Broad database support
features.description: Superset connects to most SQL-speaking databases and warehouses, including Postgres, MySQL, BigQuery, Snowflake, and Redshift.

features.title: Semantic layer
features.description: A lightweight metrics layer lets teams define reusable calculations and dimensions across dashboards.

features.title: Enterprise authentication
features.description: Built-in support for OAuth, OpenID Connect, and LDAP enables integration with existing identity systems.

features.title: Row-level security
features.description: Access rules can restrict which rows or datasets individual users or roles are allowed to see.

features.title: Visualization plugins
features.description: A plugin architecture and Apache ECharts integration allow teams to extend the built-in chart library.

features.title: Dashboard sharing and embedding
features.description: Dashboards can be shared internally or embedded into other applications for wider distribution.

pros: Completely free and open source with no per-seat licensing cost
Backed by the Apache Software Foundation with an active open-source community
Connects to a very wide range of SQL databases and data warehouses
Highly extensible through plugins and a semantic layer
No vendor lock-in since the project and its source code are fully open
cons: Self-hosting requires engineering effort to deploy, secure, and maintain
No official vendor support channel for the free open-source version
Setup and configuration are more technical than fully managed BI tools
Feature velocity and documentation quality can vary release to release since it is community-driven
Teams wanting a hosted, supported version must adopt a separate commercial product like Preset

faqs.question: Is Apache Superset free
faqs.answer: Yes, Apache Superset is free and open source under the Apache License 2.0; you only pay for the infrastructure you host it on.

faqs.question: Who created Apache Superset
faqs.answer: Superset was created by Maxime Beauchemin at Airbnb in 2015 as a hackathon project originally named Caravel.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Superset and Preset
faqs.answer: Superset is the free, open-source project governed by the Apache Software Foundation, while Preset is a separate commercial company that sells a managed, hosted version of Superset as a paid SaaS product.

faqs.question: What databases does Superset support
faqs.answer: Superset connects to most SQL-speaking databases and data warehouses, including Postgres, MySQL, BigQuery, Snowflake, and Amazon Redshift, among many others.

faqs.question: Can Superset handle large-scale data
faqs.answer: Yes, Superset is designed to handle data exploration and visualization at large scale, including petabyte-scale datasets when connected to an appropriate warehouse.

faqs.question: Does Superset support single sign-on
faqs.answer: Yes, Superset supports enterprise authentication methods including OAuth, OpenID Connect, and LDAP.

faqs.question: Is Superset a top-level Apache project
faqs.answer: Yes, Superset graduated from the Apache Incubator and became a top-level Apache Software Foundation project in 2021.

faqs.question: Do I need coding skills to use Superset
faqs.answer: Basic dashboard building can be done through the no-code chart builder, but self-hosting, advanced SQL queries, and custom visualizations typically require technical skills.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted BI for engineering-led teams
use_cases.description: Data and platform engineering teams deploy Superset internally to give analysts and stakeholders dashboards without paying per-seat BI licensing fees.

use_cases.title: SQL-native ad hoc analysis
use_cases.description: Analysts use SQL Lab within Superset to explore warehouse data directly and turn queries into shareable charts and dashboards.

use_cases.title: Embedded analytics in internal tools
use_cases.description: Engineering teams embed Superset dashboards inside internal applications or customer-facing products to surface data without building custom visualization tooling.

enrichment.short_description: SuperTokens is an open-source user authentication platform that offers prebuilt login flows, session management, and either self-hosted or managed cloud deployment.
enrichment.starting_price: Free (self-hosted, or cloud usage under 5,000 monthly active users)
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SuperTokens overview covering open-source authentication features, self-hosted vs cloud pricing, and how it compares to Auth0 and Firebase Auth.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: SuperTokens is an open-source user authentication platform founded in 2019 by Rishabh Poddar and Advait Ruia, headquartered in San Francisco, California, with an additional office in Mumbai, India. It is positioned as an open-source alternative to proprietary identity providers such as Auth0, Firebase Auth, and AWS Cognito. SuperTokens offers email and password login, passwordless login via magic links or OTP, and social and OAuth 2.0 login with providers like Google, GitHub, and Facebook, along with session management, role-based access control, a user management dashboard, and multi-factor authentication. It can be self-hosted for free at any scale using the open-source core, or run through SuperTokens' managed cloud service, which is free for up to 5,000 monthly active users and then billed at 0.02 dollars per MAU with a 100 dollar monthly minimum once that threshold is crossed. Paid add-ons include multi-factor authentication, account linking, a per-seat dashboard user fee, multi-tenancy, and machine-to-machine authentication, most priced separately from the core MAU fee. The company is backed by Y Combinator and reported roughly 5 million dollars in revenue with a small team. SuperTokens is aimed at engineering teams that want full control over authentication infrastructure and user data without vendor lock-in.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is SuperTokens
paragraphs: SuperTokens is an open-source authentication and session management platform built as an alternative to closed-source identity providers. Founded in 2019 by Rishabh Poddar and Advait Ruia and headquartered in San Francisco with a Mumbai office, the company positions its product as a way for engineering teams to own their authentication stack instead of depending entirely on a third-party vendor.

The platform can be deployed in two ways: fully self-hosted on a team's own infrastructure using the open-source core at no cost, or run through SuperTokens' managed cloud service, which handles hosting, scaling, and reliability for a usage-based fee.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Core authentication methods include email and password login, passwordless login through magic links or one-time codes sent by SMS or email, and social and OAuth 2.0 login with providers such as Google, GitHub, and Facebook. The platform also handles email verification and password reset flows out of the box.

Beyond login itself, SuperTokens provides session management, a user management dashboard, role-based access control, multi-factor authentication, and prebuilt sign-up and sign-in UI components, along with hooks and overrides that let developers customize behavior at the code level. Backend SDKs are available for Node.js, Python, and Go, with frontend support for vanilla JavaScript, React, and React Native.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Self-hosting the open-source core is free at any scale, with no cap on the number of users, since teams run it on their own database and infrastructure. SuperTokens' managed cloud offering is free for up to 5,000 monthly active users, then billed at 0.02 dollars per MAU with a 100 dollar monthly minimum once that threshold is exceeded.

Paid add-ons apply on top of the base plan for both self-hosted and cloud deployments, including multi-factor authentication at 0.01 dollars per MAU, account linking at 0.005 dollars per MAU, and dashboard users at 20 dollars per user per month with three free. Multi-tenancy and machine-to-machine authentication are priced through custom sales quotes.

features.title: Email and password login
features.description: Standard credential-based authentication with built-in email verification and password reset flows.

features.title: Passwordless login
features.description: Magic link and one-time password login via SMS or email removes the need for users to manage passwords.

features.title: Social and OAuth login
features.description: Prebuilt integrations support login with Google, GitHub, Facebook, and other OAuth 2.0 providers.

features.title: Session management
features.description: Secure, rotating session tokens are handled automatically to protect against common session attacks.

features.title: Multi-factor authentication
features.description: An optional MFA add-on adds an extra verification step for higher-security applications.

features.title: User management dashboard
features.description: A built-in admin dashboard lets teams view, search, and manage user accounts without custom tooling.

features.title: Role-based access control
features.description: RBAC lets teams assign permissions and roles to users directly within the authentication layer.

features.title: Self-hosted or managed cloud
features.description: Teams can run SuperTokens entirely on their own infrastructure or offload hosting to the managed cloud service.

pros: Open-source core is free to self-host at unlimited scale with full data ownership
Cloud plan has a generous free tier of up to 5,000 monthly active users
Broad SDK coverage across Node.js, Python, Go, React, and React Native
Avoids vendor lock-in compared to closed-source identity providers
Backed by Y Combinator with active development and community support
cons: Self-hosting requires engineering time to deploy, secure, and maintain infrastructure
Several useful features like multi-factor authentication and account linking are paid add-ons rather than included
Multi-tenancy and machine-to-machine auth pricing require contacting sales rather than transparent self-serve pricing
Smaller team and community than large incumbents like Auth0 may mean slower support response for edge cases
Dashboard user pricing at 20 dollars per user per month can add up for larger internal teams

faqs.question: Is SuperTokens free
faqs.answer: Yes, the open-source core is free to self-host at any scale, and the managed cloud version is free for up to 5,000 monthly active users.

faqs.question: Who founded SuperTokens
faqs.answer: SuperTokens was founded in 2019 by Rishabh Poddar and Advait Ruia and is headquartered in San Francisco, California.

faqs.question: How does SuperTokens pricing work
faqs.answer: Self-hosted use is free at unlimited scale; the managed cloud service charges 0.02 dollars per monthly active user above a 5,000 MAU free tier, with a 100 dollar monthly minimum once billing starts, plus optional paid add-ons.

faqs.question: What login methods does SuperTokens support
faqs.answer: SuperTokens supports email and password login, passwordless login via magic link or OTP, and social and OAuth 2.0 login with providers like Google, GitHub, and Facebook.

faqs.question: Is SuperTokens an alternative to Auth0
faqs.answer: Yes, SuperTokens is commonly positioned as an open-source alternative to Auth0, Firebase Auth, and AWS Cognito.

faqs.question: Which programming languages does SuperTokens support
faqs.answer: Backend SDKs cover Node.js, Python, and Go, with frontend support for vanilla JavaScript, React, and React Native.

faqs.question: Can SuperTokens be self-hosted
faqs.answer: Yes, the open-source core can be self-hosted on a team's own infrastructure and database at no licensing cost.

faqs.question: Does SuperTokens support multi-factor authentication
faqs.answer: Yes, multi-factor authentication is available as a paid add-on priced per monthly active user.

use_cases.title: Custom authentication for SaaS products
use_cases.description: Engineering teams building a SaaS product use SuperTokens to add login, session management, and MFA without building it from scratch or fully outsourcing to a closed vendor.

use_cases.title: Data-sovereign self-hosted auth
use_cases.description: Companies with strict data residency or compliance requirements self-host SuperTokens so user credentials never leave their own infrastructure.
use_cases.audience: Regulated industries and enterprises with compliance needs

use_cases.title: Replacing legacy or expensive identity providers
use_cases.description: Teams migrating off costly proprietary identity platforms use SuperTokens to reduce per-user authentication costs while keeping similar functionality.

pricing_plans.price: Free under 5,000 MAU, then 0.02 dollars per MAU (100 dollar monthly minimum)
features: Same core authentication features as self-hosted

enrichment.short_description: SupportBee is an email-first help desk platform that gives small teams a shared inbox, knowledge base, and customer portal for managing support tickets collaboratively.
enrichment.seo_title: SupportBee Review: Help Desk Pricing and Features
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SupportBee review covering its shared inbox, knowledge base, customer portal, pricing plans, and pros and cons for small support teams.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: SupportBee is an email-first help desk platform built around a shared inbox rather than a traditional complex ticketing dashboard, aimed at small teams that handle customer support primarily through email. It was founded by Hana Mohan and Nithya Rajaram in the early 2010s. Its core features include a shared inbox for collaborative email handling, a knowledge base and customer portal, saved reply snippets, customer satisfaction ratings, filters, basic and enterprise-tier integrations, API access, and unlimited email inboxes and tickets on every plan with no per-ticket charges. SupportBee is priced on two subscription tiers: Startup at 17 dollars per seat per month billed annually (20 dollars per seat per month billed monthly), and Enterprise at 21 dollars per seat per month billed annually (25 dollars per seat per month billed monthly), with Enterprise adding the customer portal, business hours, enterprise integrations, and audit log and data history. Both plans include a 14-day free trial and can be cancelled anytime without penalty. SupportBee positions itself against more complex help desk suites by emphasizing simplicity, transparent per-seat pricing, and a familiar email-based workflow, making it a fit for small support teams and startups that find larger platforms like Zendesk or Freshdesk overbuilt for their needs.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is SupportBee
paragraphs: SupportBee is a help desk platform built around a shared inbox model, letting small teams manage customer support email collaboratively without the complexity of a large enterprise ticketing suite. It was co-founded by Hana Mohan and Nithya Rajaram in the early 2010s.

Rather than forcing every conversation through rigid ticket workflows, SupportBee keeps the experience close to email while adding collaboration features like internal notes, saved reply snippets, and a knowledge base, which appeals to small teams that want simplicity over configurability.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: SupportBee's shared inbox lets multiple agents see and respond to the same set of customer emails, with internal discussion threads for behind-the-scenes collaboration before replying to a customer. Snippets store canned responses for common questions, and filters help route or organize incoming messages.

The Enterprise plan adds a customer-facing portal where customers can view and track their own tickets, along with business hours configuration, deeper third-party integrations, and audit log and data history for compliance-minded teams. All plans include unlimited email inboxes and tickets with no per-ticket charges, plus API access for building custom workflows.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: SupportBee offers two plans, both billed per seat. The Startup plan costs 17 dollars per seat per month when billed annually, or 20 dollars per seat per month billed monthly, and includes the shared inbox, snippets, one knowledge base, filters, basic integrations, and API access. The Enterprise plan costs 21 dollars per seat per month billed annually, or 25 dollars per seat per month billed monthly, adding the customer portal, business hours, enterprise integrations, and audit log and data history.
Both plans include a 14-day free trial, unlimited tickets and inboxes with no hidden per-ticket fees, and subscriptions can be cancelled at any time without penalty.

features.title: Shared inbox
features.description: Multiple agents can view, discuss, and respond to the same customer emails from a single collaborative inbox.

features.title: Reply snippets
features.description: Saved canned responses speed up replies to frequently asked questions.

features.title: Knowledge base
features.description: A self-service knowledge base lets customers find answers without opening a ticket.

features.title: Customer portal
features.description: On the Enterprise plan, customers can log in to track the status of their own support tickets.

features.title: Customer satisfaction ratings
features.description: Built-in CSAT surveys let teams measure support quality directly from resolved tickets.

features.title: Filters and routing
features.description: Filters help organize and route incoming support emails to the right agents or inboxes.

features.title: API access
features.description: All plans include API access for integrating SupportBee with other internal tools and workflows.

features.title: Unlimited inboxes and tickets
features.description: Every plan supports unlimited email inboxes and ticket volume with no per-ticket charges.

pros: Simple, email-first workflow that is easy for small teams to adopt quickly
Transparent per-seat pricing with no hidden per-ticket fees
Unlimited tickets and inboxes included on every plan
14-day free trial and no-penalty cancellation lower the risk of trying it
Lighter weight and less complex to configure than large enterprise help desk suites
cons: Fewer advanced automation and workflow features compared to larger platforms like Zendesk or Freshdesk
Customer portal is only available on the higher-priced Enterprise plan
Public information on company headquarters and team size is limited and inconsistent across sources
Smaller integration ecosystem than category leaders
May not scale well for large support organizations with complex routing needs

faqs.question: What is SupportBee used for
faqs.answer: SupportBee is a help desk platform used by small teams to manage customer support email collaboratively through a shared inbox, knowledge base, and customer portal.

faqs.question: How much does SupportBee cost
faqs.answer: SupportBee costs 17 dollars per seat per month billed annually on the Startup plan, and 21 dollars per seat per month billed annually on the Enterprise plan.

faqs.question: Does SupportBee offer a free trial
faqs.answer: Yes, both SupportBee plans include a 14-day free trial.

faqs.question: Does SupportBee charge per ticket
faqs.answer: No, all plans include unlimited email inboxes and tickets with no per-ticket charges.

faqs.question: What is the difference between SupportBee Startup and Enterprise plans
faqs.answer: The Enterprise plan adds a customer portal, business hours configuration, enterprise-grade integrations, and audit log and data history on top of everything in the Startup plan.

faqs.question: Who founded SupportBee
faqs.answer: SupportBee was co-founded by Hana Mohan and Nithya Rajaram.

faqs.question: Can I cancel SupportBee anytime
faqs.answer: Yes, SupportBee subscriptions can be cancelled at any time without penalty.

faqs.question: Is SupportBee good for small businesses
faqs.answer: Yes, SupportBee is designed for small support teams that want a simple, email-based help desk without the complexity of larger enterprise platforms.

use_cases.title: Small team email support
use_cases.description: Small businesses use SupportBee's shared inbox to manage customer emails collaboratively without juggling separate personal inboxes.

use_cases.title: Self-service knowledge base
use_cases.description: Support teams publish a knowledge base alongside their inbox so customers can resolve common questions without contacting an agent.

use_cases.title: Customer-facing ticket tracking
use_cases.description: Enterprise-plan customers give their own clients a portal to check ticket status, reducing repeat status-check emails.

enrichment.short_description: SuprSend is a notification infrastructure platform that lets developers and product teams design, send, and manage multi-channel notifications through a single API.
enrichment.seo_title: SuprSend Review: Notification API Pricing and Features
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SuprSend review covering its notification infrastructure API, multi-channel workflows, pricing plans, and pros and cons for product and engineering teams.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: SuprSend is a notification infrastructure platform founded in January 2022 by Nikita Navral and Gaurav Varma, headquartered in San Francisco, California. It gives engineering and product teams a single API and workflow builder to design, trigger, and manage transactional and marketing-style notifications across channels including email, SMS, WhatsApp, mobile push, web push, Slack, Microsoft Teams, in-app inbox, and webhooks, without building and maintaining separate integrations per channel. Key capabilities include a no-code workflow builder, a customizable in-app notification inbox with drop-in React components, template management with versioning and A/B testing, batching and digest logic, timezone-aware sending, user preference centers, and AI features such as an MCP server and agent SDK. SuprSend prices on notification and monthly tracked user volume rather than API request count: a Free plan covers up to 10,000 notifications per month, Essentials starts around 100 to 110 dollars per month for 50,000 notifications, Business starts around 250 to 275 dollars per month, and Enterprise is custom-priced with features like multi-tenant data modeling, SAML SSO, HIPAA compliance, dedicated infrastructure, and a Bring Your Own Cloud deployment option. The company has raised seed funding, including a 1 million dollar round led by BoldCap, and serves customers such as CrazyGames, Teachmint, and Freightify. SuprSend is best suited for product and engineering teams that want to centralize multi-channel notification logic instead of managing it themselves.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is SuprSend
paragraphs: SuprSend is a notification infrastructure platform founded in January 2022 by Nikita Navral and Gaurav Varma and headquartered in San Francisco, California. It is built for product and engineering teams that need to send transactional and engagement notifications across many channels without maintaining a separate integration for each one.

Instead of writing custom logic for email, SMS, push, and chat notifications individually, teams design a notification once inside SuprSend and route it to whichever channels are relevant per user, using a single API and a shared set of templates and workflows.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: SuprSend supports email, SMS, WhatsApp, mobile push, web push, Slack, Microsoft Teams, an in-app notification inbox, and webhooks from one platform. Its workflow builder lets non-technical product managers configure multi-step notification logic, branching, and conditions outside of code, while a WYSIWYG template engine supports versioning, A/B testing, and internationalization.

More advanced functionality includes batching and digest delivery, timezone-aware sending, a user preference center for granular opt-in and opt-out control, smart channel routing, and multi-tenant data modeling for SaaS platforms serving many customer organizations. The platform also offers AI features such as an MCP server, an agent SDK, and an AI co-pilot for building workflows.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: SuprSend prices plans by notification volume and monthly tracked users rather than by API request count. The Free plan covers up to 10,000 notifications per month with no credit card required. The Essentials plan starts at roughly 100 to 110 dollars per month for 50,000 notifications, with overage priced per additional thousand notifications. The Business plan starts at roughly 250 to 275 dollars per month and adds batching and digest, user preferences, and data modeling for objects.

The Enterprise plan is custom-priced and adds multi-tenant data modeling, audit trail and role-based access control, SAML 2.0 single sign-on, HIPAA and BAA compliance, dedicated infrastructure, and a self-hosted or Bring Your Own Cloud deployment option for teams with strict data residency requirements.

features.title: Single API for all channels
features.description: One API call can trigger notifications across email, SMS, WhatsApp, push, Slack, Teams, and in-app inbox.

features.title: No-code workflow builder
features.description: Product managers can configure multi-step notification workflows and branching logic without writing code.

features.title: In-app notification inbox
features.description: A customizable, real-time in-app inbox ships with drop-in React components or a headless API for custom UI.

features.title: Template engine
features.description: A WYSIWYG editor supports template versioning, A/B testing, and multi-language content.

features.title: Batching and digest
features.description: Notifications can be grouped into digests to avoid overwhelming users with individual alerts.

features.title: User preference center
features.description: End users can control which notification types and channels they receive through a built-in preference center.

features.title: Multi-tenant data modeling
features.description: SaaS platforms serving many customer organizations can model notification data per tenant.

features.title: AI-assisted workflows
features.description: An MCP server, agent SDK, and AI co-pilot help teams build and automate notification workflows faster.

pros: Free plan covers up to 10,000 notifications per month with no credit card required
Single API and workflow builder cover essentially every major notification channel
In-app inbox ships with ready-made React components to speed up integration
Enterprise tier supports HIPAA compliance and self-hosted or Bring Your Own Cloud deployment
Backed by seed funding and used by recognizable customers like CrazyGames and Teachmint
cons: As a company founded in 2022 with a small team, its long-term track record is shorter than legacy notification vendors
Advanced features like batching, digests, and data modeling for objects are gated behind the Business plan and above
Enterprise features such as SSO and HIPAA compliance require a custom quote rather than transparent pricing
Pricing model based on notification and tracked-user volume requires forecasting usage to avoid overage costs
Smaller ecosystem and community compared to older, larger notification platforms

faqs.answer: SuprSend was founded in January 2022 by Nikita Navral and Gaurav Varma and is headquartered in San Francisco, California.

faqs.answer: Yes, the Enterprise plan offers self-hosted and Bring Your Own Cloud deployment options for teams with strict data residency needs.

enrichment.short_description: Surfer is a lightweight, open-source static file server packaged for Cloudron, letting self-hosters serve static websites and share files through a web interface, CLI, or WebDAV.
enrichment.seo_title: Surfer (Cloudron): Static Site and File Hosting Overview
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Overview of Surfer, the open-source static file server app for Cloudron, covering its features, deployment model, and how it differs from Surfer SEO.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Surfer is an open-source static file server packaged as an app for the Cloudron self-hosting platform, maintained in the Cloudron GitLab and GitHub organizations. It is not related to Surfer SEO, the content optimization SaaS company; Surfer the Cloudron app is instead a simple tool for serving static websites and sharing files from a self-hosted server. It provides a web-based admin interface (typically available at a slash underscore admin path on the app domain), a command-line tool for uploading files from local folders, and WebDAV support so files can be mounted and managed directly through a desktop file manager on Windows, Mac, or Linux. Additional capabilities include automatic serving of index.html or index.htm files, optional public directory listing, custom 404 error pages, and three access control modes covering fully public access, password protection, or Cloudron user login. Because it has a very low resource footprint, the recommended deployment pattern is to run one Surfer instance per domain so that separate static sites remain isolated from each other. As an open-source, self-hosted Cloudron package rather than a commercial company, Surfer has no pricing tiers, no publicly documented founding date, headquarters, or employee count, and is used primarily by individuals and small teams already running a Cloudron server who need simple static site or file hosting without paying for a separate managed service.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Surfer
paragraphs: Surfer is a simple, open-source static file server distributed as a packaged app for Cloudron, a self-hosting platform for running web applications on your own server. It is maintained in Cloudron's own GitLab and GitHub repositories rather than by a standalone commercial company.

This Surfer should not be confused with Surfer SEO, a separate and unrelated content optimization SaaS company based in Poland. The Cloudron Surfer app has no connection to Surfer SEO's product, team, pricing, or business, and the two only happen to share a short product name.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Surfer is designed for hosting static websites and sharing files from a self-hosted Cloudron instance. Files can be managed three ways: through a web-based admin interface, a command-line tool that uploads files from local folders, or a WebDAV endpoint that lets a desktop file manager mount the server as a network drive.

The app automatically serves an index.html or index.htm file for a given directory, can optionally show a directory listing for folders without an index file, and supports a custom 404.html page for handling missing pages. Access to each Surfer instance can be configured as fully public, password protected, or restricted to logged-in Cloudron users, and its command-line tool supports scripted deployment for basic continuous integration workflows.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Surfer is free and open source. Since it runs as an app inside a self-hosted Cloudron installation, there is no separate license fee for the Surfer package itself; the only cost is whatever a user already pays for their Cloudron server and hosting infrastructure.

There are no paid tiers, add-ons, or usage-based charges specific to Surfer, which is consistent with its role as a lightweight utility app in the broader Cloudron app store rather than a standalone commercial product.

features.title: Web-based admin interface
features.description: Files and folders can be uploaded and managed directly through a browser-based admin panel.

features.title: Command-line upload tool
features.description: A CLI tool lets users push files from a local folder to the server, useful for scripted or automated deployments.

features.title: WebDAV support
features.description: Surfer exposes a WebDAV endpoint so it can be mounted as a network drive in desktop file managers on Windows, Mac, and Linux.

features.title: Automatic index file serving
features.description: The server automatically serves index.html or index.htm for a directory, matching standard static site behavior.

features.title: Optional directory listing
features.description: Folders without an index file can optionally show a public listing of their contents.

features.title: Custom error pages
features.description: A custom 404.html page can be configured to handle requests for missing files or pages.

features.title: Flexible access control
features.description: Each instance can be set to fully public access, password protection, or restricted to logged-in Cloudron users.

features.title: Low resource footprint
features.description: Surfer is lightweight enough that running one instance per domain for isolation is a practical, low-overhead pattern.

pros: Free and open source with no licensing cost beyond existing Cloudron hosting
Multiple file management options, including web UI, CLI, and WebDAV
Very low resource footprint makes it practical to run per-domain instances
Simple to deploy for anyone already running a Cloudron server
Flexible access control ranging from public to password-protected to login-restricted
cons: Requires an existing Cloudron installation, so it is not usable as a standalone hosted product
Very limited public documentation on founding history, company backing, or roadmap
Lacks the advanced build tooling, CDN, and deployment automation of dedicated static hosting platforms
Small niche user base compared to mainstream static hosting services
Frequently confused with the unrelated Surfer SEO product due to the identical short name

faqs.answer: No, Surfer the Cloudron app is completely unrelated to Surfer SEO, a separate content optimization SaaS company; they only share a similar short name.

enrichment.short_description: Surfer SEO is a cloud-based content optimization platform that combines SERP analysis, an AI content editor, keyword research, and AI search visibility tracking to help teams create pages that rank.
enrichment.seo_title: Surfer SEO Review 2026: Pricing, Features and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Surfer SEO review covering content optimization features, AI search tracking, pricing tiers, and pros and cons for SEO teams and content creators.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Surfer SEO is a cloud-based content optimization platform founded in 2017 by Michal Suski and Tomasz Niezgoda and headquartered in Wroclaw, Poland. It is not related to the Cloudron Surfer static file server despite sharing a short name. In January 2025 Surfer SEO was acquired by Groupe Positive, a European SaaS provider based in Lille, France, and the product is now marketed under the name Positive Surfer. Surfer SEO analyzes top-ranking search results and provides data-driven content guidelines, keyword research, an AI writing and optimization editor, a plagiarism checker, an AI humanizer, content audits, topical mapping, and internal linking recommendations, alongside newer AI search visibility tracking across ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI features, and Gemini. It integrates with WordPress, Google Docs, Contentful, and Zapier. As of 2026, pricing runs on five tiers billed annually: Discovery at 49 euros per month, Standard at 99 euros per month, Pro at 182 euros per month, Peace of Mind at 299 euros per month, and a custom-priced Enterprise tier from roughly 999 euros per month with SSO and white-label options. The company reports being used by more than 150,000 content creators, SEO professionals, and agencies worldwide, and every plan includes a 7-day money-back guarantee. Surfer SEO is best suited for content teams, SEO agencies, and marketers who want quantitative, SERP-based guidance for writing and optimizing pages that rank in both traditional and AI-powered search.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Surfer SEO
paragraphs: Surfer SEO is a cloud-based content optimization platform founded in 2017 by Michal Suski and Tomasz Niezgoda in Wroclaw, Poland. It started as a tool for analyzing top-ranking search results and has grown into a broader AI-driven content optimization suite used by more than 150,000 content creators, SEO professionals, and marketing agencies.

In January 2025, Surfer SEO was acquired by Groupe Positive, a European SaaS provider headquartered in Lille, France, and the product is now also marketed under the name Positive Surfer while retaining the Surfer SEO brand for its core content optimization tools.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: The core of Surfer SEO is its content editor, which scores a draft against data pulled from top-ranking pages for a target keyword and gives quantitative guidelines on word count, headings, keyword usage, and related terms. It also includes keyword research, topical mapping, content gap and cannibalization analysis, and an internal linking tool.

More recently, Surfer SEO has expanded into AI search visibility, tracking how a brand and its content appear in AI answer engines like ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI features, and Gemini. Other features include a plagiarism checker, an AI humanizer, an AI writing assistant called Surfy, and native integrations with WordPress, Google Docs, Contentful, and Zapier.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Surfer SEO is sold on five annually-billed tiers. Discovery starts at 49 euros per month with 120 documents and tracking for 10 pages. Standard is 99 euros per month with 360 documents and weekly-refreshed AI prompt tracking. Pro is 182 euros per month, the most commonly recommended tier, with daily-refreshed multi-AI tracking across ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google, and Gemini, plus internal linking and content gap analysis.

The Peace of Mind plan is 299 euros per month with unlimited documents, 100 tracked AI prompts refreshed daily, advanced SERP analysis, a dedicated success manager, and API access. Enterprise starts around 999 euros per month with custom limits, white-label options, SSO, and an advisory program. Every plan includes a 7-day money-back guarantee.

features.title: SERP-based content editor
features.description: Drafts are scored against real-time data from top-ranking pages, with guidance on word count, headings, and keyword usage.

features.title: Keyword research
features.description: Built-in keyword research tools help identify target terms and related topics to cover in content.

features.title: AI search visibility tracking
features.description: Tracks how content and brand mentions appear across AI answer engines including ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI features, and Gemini.

features.title: Content audit
features.description: Existing pages can be audited and re-optimized against current SERP data to improve rankings.

features.title: Topical mapping
features.description: Helps teams plan clusters of related content around a core topic to build topical authority.

features.title: Internal linking and cannibalization reports
features.description: Identifies internal linking opportunities and flags pages competing for the same keywords.

features.title: Plagiarism checker and AI humanizer
features.description: Built-in checks help ensure originality and reduce AI-sounding phrasing in generated drafts.

features.title: CMS and workflow integrations
features.description: Native integrations with WordPress, Google Docs, Contentful, and Zapier fit Surfer into existing publishing workflows.

pros: Quantitative, data-driven content guidelines based on real top-ranking search results
Expanded AI search visibility tracking keeps pace with the shift toward AI answer engines
Wide range of features spanning research, writing, auditing, and internal linking in one platform
Native integrations with popular CMS and workflow tools like WordPress and Google Docs
7-day money-back guarantee on every plan reduces the risk of trying it
cons: Entry-level Discovery plan has a fairly limited document allowance for active content teams
Higher tiers needed for daily AI prompt tracking and multi-AI coverage can get expensive
Content scores can encourage keyword-driven writing that some teams find overly formulaic
Recently acquired by Groupe Positive, adding some uncertainty around long-term branding and roadmap changes
Enterprise-grade features like SSO and white-labeling require the highest, custom-priced tier

faqs.question: What is Surfer SEO used for
faqs.answer: Surfer SEO is used to optimize written content for search rankings by scoring drafts against data from top-ranking pages and providing keyword, structure, and internal linking guidance.

faqs.question: How much does Surfer SEO cost
faqs.answer: As of 2026, plans range from 49 euros per month for Discovery to 299 euros per month for Peace of Mind, with a custom-priced Enterprise tier starting around 999 euros per month, all billed annually.

faqs.question: Who founded Surfer SEO
faqs.answer: Surfer SEO was founded in 2017 by Michal Suski and Tomasz Niezgoda in Wroclaw, Poland.

faqs.question: Was Surfer SEO acquired
faqs.answer: Yes, Surfer SEO was acquired by Groupe Positive, a European SaaS provider based in Lille, France, in January 2025.

faqs.question: Does Surfer SEO track AI search results
faqs.answer: Yes, higher-tier plans track brand and content visibility across AI answer engines including ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI features, and Gemini.

faqs.question: Does Surfer SEO integrate with WordPress
faqs.answer: Yes, Surfer SEO offers native integrations with WordPress, Google Docs, Contentful, and Zapier.

faqs.question: Is Surfer SEO the same as the Cloudron app called Surfer
faqs.answer: No, Surfer SEO is a completely separate and unrelated product from the Cloudron Surfer static file server; they only share a similar short name.

faqs.question: Does Surfer SEO offer a free trial or refund
faqs.answer: Surfer SEO does not typically offer a free plan, but every paid plan includes a 7-day money-back guarantee.

enrichment.short_description: Surfshark is a consumer VPN service offering unlimited simultaneous device connections, ad blocking, and optional antivirus and identity protection add-ons under a no-logs privacy policy.
enrichment.starting_price: 2.29 euros/month (2-year Starter plan, plus bonus months)
enrichment.seo_title: Surfshark VPN Review 2026: Pricing, Features and Plans
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Surfshark VPN review covering pricing plans, server network, security features, antivirus and identity protection add-ons, and pros and cons.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Surfshark is a consumer VPN and digital privacy service founded in 2018 and headquartered in Amsterdam, Netherlands, with additional offices in Vilnius and Kaunas, Lithuania, Warsaw, Poland, and Berlin, Germany. In 2022 Surfshark merged with Nord Security, though the two brands continue to operate as separate products with independent apps, server infrastructure, and pricing. Surfshark operates a network of more than 4,500 servers across roughly 100 countries and allows unlimited simultaneous device connections on every plan, which differentiates it from many competing VPN services that cap device counts. Core features include AES encryption and IP masking, an ad and cookie pop-up blocker, rotating IP addresses, and a double VPN mode for extra encryption layers. The service is sold on three subscription tiers billed mainly as 24-month plans with bonus months: Starter (VPN plus ad blocking), Surfshark One (adds an antivirus suite), and Surfshark One+ (adds Surfshark Alert identity and data breach monitoring, plus Incogni personal data removal). Starter pricing begins around 2.29 euros per month on the long-term plan, with higher rates for shorter one-month or one-year commitments. All plans include a 30-day money-back guarantee and a free trial period. Surfshark's no-logs policy has been independently audited, and the company reports over 4,500 servers across around 100 countries. It is best suited for individuals and families who want unlimited-device VPN coverage plus optional bundled antivirus or identity protection.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Surfshark
paragraphs: Surfshark is a consumer VPN and digital privacy service founded in 2018 and headquartered in Amsterdam, Netherlands, with additional offices in Lithuania, Poland, and Germany. In 2022 the company merged with Nord Security, though Surfshark continues to operate as an independent product with its own apps, server network, and pricing.

Surfshark differentiates itself from many VPN competitors by allowing unlimited simultaneous device connections on every subscription, making a single plan usable across an entire household or every device an individual owns.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: The core VPN service includes strong encryption and IP masking across a network of more than 4,500 servers in roughly 100 countries, along with an ad and cookie pop-up blocker, rotating IP addresses, and a double VPN mode that routes traffic through two servers for additional encryption. Surfshark's no-logs policy has been independently audited.

Beyond the VPN itself, Surfshark bundles optional privacy tools into its higher tiers, including Surfshark Alternative ID for masked emails and generated personas, an antivirus suite with real-time protection, Surfshark Alert for data breach monitoring, an ad-free private search tool, and identity theft coverage up to 1 million dollars for United States residents, along with the Incogni data removal service.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Surfshark offers three subscription tiers: Starter, Surfshark One, and Surfshark One+, each available on monthly, one-year, or two-year billing with the lowest per-month rate on the two-year plan plus bonus months. Starter pricing begins around 2.29 euros per month on the long-term plan and covers full VPN access, ad blocking, and cookie pop-up blocking.

Surfshark One adds a bundled antivirus suite for a modest price increase, while Surfshark One+ adds Surfshark Alert identity protection and Incogni data removal on top of everything in the lower tiers. All plans include a 30-day money-back guarantee, and pricing varies by currency, region, and active promotional discounts.

features.title: Unlimited device connections
features.description: Every plan allows unlimited simultaneous device connections, unlike many competing VPN services that cap device counts.

features.title: Large server network
features.description: More than 4,500 servers across roughly 100 countries support fast, geographically diverse connections.

features.title: Ad and cookie pop-up blocker
features.description: Built-in blocking reduces ads and cookie consent pop-ups while browsing.

features.title: Double VPN encryption
features.description: Traffic can be routed through two servers for an additional layer of encryption.

features.title: Independently audited no-logs policy
features.description: Surfshark's claim of not logging user activity has been verified through independent audits.

features.title: Antivirus suite (Surfshark One)
features.description: Real-time antivirus protection is bundled into the Surfshark One and One+ tiers.

features.title: Identity and data breach monitoring
features.description: Surfshark Alert on the One+ tier monitors for data breaches involving a user's personal information.

features.title: Data removal service
features.description: The Incogni integration on One+ helps remove personal data from data broker sites.

pros: Unlimited simultaneous device connections on every plan
Large server network spanning roughly 100 countries
Independently audited no-logs privacy policy
Bundled antivirus and identity protection options for users who want more than just a VPN
30-day money-back guarantee lowers the risk of trying the service
cons: Lowest advertised prices require committing to a 24-month plan; monthly pricing is significantly higher
Antivirus and identity protection add-ons raise the price compared to VPN-only competitors
Merger with Nord Security in 2022 has raised some user questions about long-term brand independence
Some advanced privacy features like identity theft coverage are limited to United States residents
Promotional pricing structures with bonus months can make true cost comparisons harder for new customers

faqs.question: What is Surfshark used for
faqs.answer: Surfshark is a VPN service used to encrypt internet traffic, mask a user's IP address, and add privacy tools like ad blocking, antivirus, and identity monitoring.

faqs.question: How much does Surfshark cost
faqs.answer: The Starter plan starts around 2.29 euros per month on the long-term two-year plan, with Surfshark One and One+ priced higher for added antivirus and identity protection features.

faqs.question: Is Surfshark owned by Nord Security
faqs.answer: Surfshark merged with Nord Security in 2022, but the two continue to operate as separate products with independent apps, infrastructure, and pricing.

faqs.answer: Yes, every Surfshark plan supports unlimited simultaneous device connections.

faqs.question: Where is Surfshark headquartered
faqs.answer: Surfshark is headquartered in Amsterdam, Netherlands, with additional offices in Lithuania, Poland, and Germany.

faqs.question: Does Surfshark keep logs of user activity
faqs.answer: No, Surfshark operates under a no-logs policy that has been independently audited.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Surfshark Starter, One, and One+
faqs.answer: Starter includes core VPN features and ad blocking, One adds an antivirus suite, and One+ adds Surfshark Alert identity monitoring and Incogni data removal.

faqs.question: Does Surfshark offer a money-back guarantee
faqs.answer: Yes, all Surfshark plans include a 30-day money-back guarantee.
